# AI Exposure of the European Job Market

## Project
An interactive treemap visualizing AI exposure across ~130 European occupation groups (ISCO-08 3-digit level) in 36 countries. Built by Philipp Maul / Nexalps.

## Methodology
- Occupation data from ESCO v1.2.1 (~3,000 occupations with rich descriptions)
- Employment statistics from Eurostat EU-LFS 2024 (ISCO-08 2-digit, distributed to 3-digit using ESCO structural weights)
- Wage data from Eurostat Structure of Earnings Survey 2022, BFS LSE 2024 (Switzerland), ONS ASHE 2025 (UK)
- AI exposure scoring via Claude Sonnet 4 (Anthropic), producing two scores per group:
  - technical_score (0-10): Pure AI capability — how much CAN AI reshape this job?
  - regulated_score (0-10): Practical European exposure, factoring in EU AI Act high-risk obligations, works council consultation, employment protection, GDPR constraints — how much WILL AI reshape this job in the EU?
  - uk_regulated_score (0-10): UK-specific regulated exposure, factoring in Equality Act 2010, UK GDPR Art 22, Employment Rights Act 1996, and the DSIT pro-innovation framework
- EU AI Act structured overlay with legal source references (Annex III categories, deployer/subject classifications, Platform Work Directive)
- Aggregated to ISCO-08 3-digit level (~130 groups) for treemap visualization

## Key Findings
- 199.6M total jobs covered (EU-27 + UK, CH, candidate countries)
- Average technical exposure: 5.6 / 10
- Average regulated exposure: 4.4 / 10
- Average regulatory delta: 1.2 points (EU regulation reduces practical AI exposure)
- UK average regulatory friction: 0.5 points (less than half the EU average)
- Exposure-weighted wages: ~€4.7T (technical) vs ~€3.7T (regulated)
- 40 occupation groups classified as high-risk AI deployers under EU AI Act
- 125 groups trigger Art 26(7) worker representation requirements
- 31 groups trigger the Platform Work Directive

## Pages
- Interactive map (index.html): Treemap visualization with country selector, score toggle, AI Act overlay
- Analysis (analysis.html): What AI exposure means for the European job market
- Questions (questions.html): Questions this data raises
- Sources (sources.html): Primary legal texts, papers, books, reports
- llms.txt: Machine-readable project summary

## Data Sources
- ESCO v1.2.1 (European Commission): https://esco.ec.europa.eu
- Eurostat EU-LFS 2024: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat
- Eurostat SES 2022 (wages): https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat
- BFS LSE 2024 (Swiss wages): https://www.bfs.admin.ch
- ONS ASHE 2025 (UK wages): https://www.ons.gov.uk
- ISCO-08 (ILO): International Standard Classification of Occupations

## Attribution
Inspired by Andrej Karpathy's AI Exposure Map (https://karpathy.ai/jobs/).
Extended with dual-score methodology for the European regulatory context, UK-specific scoring, and structured EU AI Act overlay.

- Code: MIT (Philipp Maul / Nexalps)
- Scored data and analysis: CC-BY 4.0 (Philipp Maul / Nexalps)
- Built on ESCO (EU Commission), Eurostat, BFS, and ONS data
